title: Oscar Mouse and the Musical Wolf Cub
summary: Oscar Mouse awakens in his cozy stone wall home at the onset of spring. After meeting a bird named Mischief, the two set out to find a breakfast of pretend berries scattered on the street. Their journey takes a heroic turn when they encounter a pack of wolves searching for their lost cub, Cubby Chickendoll. Despite the wolves suggesting they fly to search, Oscar and Mischief use their feet to track the cub to a peculiar music shop called Yestalam, which sells both instruments and vegetables. They find the tiny cub hiding under a violin and safely return him to his family. After helping several other friends repair their broken belongings with glue, Oscar and Mischief return to their burrow to snuggle up for a well-earned rest.
audioFilename: Stars and stories - wolf cub rescue.m4a

highlightQuote.text: Oscar and the bird walked because they have feet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time there was an old stone wall. And it was a quiet and a big and a mossy stone wall.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It had been a cold winter with lots of snow and ice. But today the sun came out, and it was the beginning of spring and it started to get warm.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And deep inside the cold stones of the stone wall, nestled among the cracks, there was a cozy burrow.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, I'm - Dad, go get the orange story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah. And who was asleep inside that little burrow inside the old stone wall?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oscar Mouse, Oscar Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oscar Mouse was asleep inside his burrow in the old stone wall. But as he was sleeping, he felt the warmth of the sun on that day when the spring was just beginning, and he heard, what did he hear?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He heard chirp, chirp, chirp, chirp, chirp, chirp, that's my burrow!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, he heard a bird chirping and saying 'that's my burrow.' And so what did Oscar do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He tried to share.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He tried to share. And so, his way of sharing was to say, 'Okay bird, you can come in the burrow with me and we can be in here together.' And what was the bird's name?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Velociraptorning.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Velociraptorning? And what was the short version of Velociraptorning's name?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Vista Lam, Mr. Caro, Mr. Laro, and Jeff. Mr. Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, at any rate, the bird with the long name said, 'Oh thank you Oscar, I would be happy to share your burrow. But I'm feeling a bit hungry for breakfast. Perhaps we could go find some food together.' And what did Oscar say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, before the next part, I need my nail clipped.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Now where were we? Oscar and the bird, what was the bird's name again?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mister Bublaganda and Brise. Mischief for short.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, Oscar and the bird, whose name may have been Mischief or may have been something else, decided to go get some breakfast. And what did they decide to go eat?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Some berries, the berries that came from the tree. The pretend berries that Mischief Mouse threw on the street for the bird.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: But those berries grew high up in a tree, so how would they get up there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, some of they were down on the street.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, some of the berries had fallen down on the street, that's true. And so Oscar Mouse and the bird, they climbed up out of their burrow and scampered down the side of the old stone wall and across the field and through the bushes and out to the sidewalk where there was a tree.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, some of them were on the street.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so they looked for some of the berries on the street, and did they find them?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And they found the berries. And then they heard a funny sound nearby, just as they started to eat. What did they hear?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Awoo! Awoo! Awoo! The wolves.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And they were just howling at the moon, so they decided to go and check out that noise when they were done with breakfast.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Right. When Oscar and the bird were done with breakfast, they decided to go and check out that noise. And what did they find?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They found a wolf just howling at the moon.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Just howling at the moon. And then the wolves, the wolves said, 'Oh hello Oscar Mouse and bird. We have a problem, maybe you can solve it for us.' What was their problem they had?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Their problem was they needed to find their tiny wolf cub. It was a baby, and the wolf cub was lost somewhere. They asked if Oscar and the bird could fly up and search.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, Oscar and the bird walked because they have feet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, they walk because they have feet to search for the baby wolf. And what was the baby wolf's name, the wolf cub?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Cub. And it was Cubby Chickendoll.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, they set out to search for Cubby Chickendoll. As they searched, they grew a little bit tired because they were walking far and wide. But then they saw a music shop.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, it was at the music shop with all the vegetables... the music instruments.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, it just so happened that Oscar Mouse and his friend the bird stumbled in to the music shop. What was the name of the music shop?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yestalam.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yestalam. And so they went right into the music shop, and they heard the musical instruments playing, but they also heard another sound, which was a tiny little...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Bark, bark, bark, bark. It was the wolf that was lost.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was the lost baby wolf cub! And so they ran in and searched around. Where was that barking coming from?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The violin.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The wolf cub was so small he was hiding under a violin. And Oscar peeked his head under and said, 'Excuse me, are you the lost wolf cub?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I am. Can we take you back to your family? Yes, but I'm afraid I'll get lost again.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, we know where to go. We'll make sure that you get back to your family safe and sound, and they'll feed you a nice big breakfast. How does that sound, wolf cub?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Good.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, let's go. So Oscar and the bird and the wolf cub, they made their way out of the music shop. And they took some vegetables along. What did they bring?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Pepper, salt, and more peppers.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Pepper, salt, and more peppers. And then they made their way back until they found the wolf pack. And what did the wolves say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We're so glad to see you, Wolf.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the wolf cub yapped happily. He said, 'Bark bark bark bark bark, mama dad, yay, I'm so happy to be home!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then what did Oscar and the bird do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Mama had a problem. Mama had to pump.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mama had to pump, that's true. She had to make milk for her baby. And then Nana had a problem.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nana's problem was she had a broken dollhouse. By gluing it back together.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow. Well, at the end of all this problem solving... and Emily had a problem.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Emily's problem was she had a broken down sculpture. She also glued it back together.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Papa had a problem.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Papa's problem was he had a broken down art supplies kit. By gluing it back together.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, back in the world of the story, Oscar and the bird made their way back home and snuggled up all nice and cozy inside their burrow.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And how about a story about Oscar now?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, I think we're done with stars and stories for now.

characters.name: Oscar Mouse
characters.description: Roused by the spring warmth of a forested green planet, this tiny mouse artisan dwells within a small house of weathered stone. Having migrated from a drafty attic to the crystalline sanctuary of a sugar can, he works deep within his dark, stony cupboard, meticulously mending every dent with the smooth application of squeak oil.

characters.name: Mischief
characters.description: Proprietor of the analog-scented tape store on the corner of Poplar and Popcornioni, this avian known as Velociraptorning is Hattie’s loyal companion and the devoted mother of Mistress Mouse. Whether aiding Oscar on his adventures from their shared burrow or tending her wall of magnetic spools, she remains a soul of simple pleasures—sketching in sun-drenched parks, sharing meals of corn, and admiring her daughter’s new hat with a prideful, artistic eye.

characters.name: Cubby Chickendoll
characters.description: A tiny wolf cub who gets lost and is eventually found hiding under a violin.

characters.name: Mama Wolf
characters.description: This silver-furred matriarch, a figure of striking grace from the story’s illustrations, bears the heavy mantle of a mother searching for her lost cub. Her amber gaze is clouded with a fierce, maternal ache as she tends to the rhythmic task of pumping milk, desperate to keep her life-giving strength ready for the moment her little one is returned to her side.

characters.name: Nana
characters.description: The dignified mole matriarch and grandmother to Hattie, Pistis, and Isabella, this pillar of watchful wisdom resides at the end of the train line, tackling life’s problems "one at a time." From engineering mouse-scale Lego structures to the meticulous restoration of dollhouses using patient glue and tiny pencils, her steady paws bridge the gap between architectural feats and delicate artistry. Whether discerning potential as a trolley passenger or welcoming weary travelers into her sanctuary, her quiet authority is so legendary that she graces the cover of Hattie’s book in a rare, peaceful slumber. Even in rest, she remains the heart of their world—a steady warmth anchoring every journey’s end, her sharp eyes softening for Izzy’s mushroom-textured crafts.

characters.name: Emily
characters.description: A steady-handed passenger on the playground trolley, this loyal companion bears the faint, tacky traces of adhesive on their fingertips—a testament to the patience required to painstakingly mend a shattered sculpture and restore beauty from its broken shards.

characters.name: Papa
characters.description: A fixture of Izzy’s household and a steadfast confidant, this family member possesses a mender’s spirit, recently evidenced by the meticulous patience they used to glue a shattered art kit back together.

characters.name: Jeff
characters.description: One of the many names mentioned for the bird.

characters.name: Mr. Caro
characters.description: One of the many names mentioned for the bird.

characters.name: Mr. Laro
characters.description: One of the many names mentioned for the bird.

places.name: Old Stone Wall
places.description: A mossy, ancient wall where Oscar Mouse lives.

places.name: The Burrow
places.description: A safe and cozy underground sanctuary for the Mischief Moles, tucked away within the protective cracks of a weathered stone wall.

places.name: The Street
places.description: The sun-dappled road where Minnie Mouse rumbles in on her bright yellow digger, pausing by the thickets to gather a harvest of pretend berries for a whimsical morning breakfast.

places.name: Yestalam
places.description: A unique music shop filled with instruments and vegetables where the lost wolf cub is found.
